new_fix_this_run.description: Wire counterfactual-resolved veto outcomes back to graduated_rules.record_outcome(). When a signal is vetoed by a graduated rule, the counterfactual_learner now tracks price action and calls record_outcome(won=pnl>0) on resolution. Previously, veto rules accumulated times_applied=N but times_correct stayed at 0 forever because no trade executed so no trade_close so record_outcome never fired. 14 lines added in update_with_price() after _maybe_write_kb_entry().
new_fix_this_run.impact: Completes the graduated rules feedback loop for vetoed signals. 4 active veto rules (hype_long_veto_v1, sol_long_veto_v1, night_session_block_v1, hype_short_veto_v1) will get accuracy tracking from next restart. Rules that are consistently wrong will auto-retire at accuracy < 35% after 10 evaluations.
new_fix_this_run.verification: python3 inspect.getsource confirmed: 'graduated_rule_veto' in skip_reason check present, get_graduated_rules_engine() called, record_outcome(won=pnl>0) called

high_priority_findings.finding: VETO_CF_WIRE_APPLIED: The missing graduated-rules feedback loop for vetoed signals has been implemented in counterfactual_learner.py. Previously, veto rules (hype_long_veto_v1, sol_long_veto_v1, night_session_block_v1, hype_short_veto_v1) accumulated times_applied=9 combined with times_correct=0 forever, making veto accuracy unmeasurable. The 14-line fix wires counterfactual resolution outcomes back to graduated_rules.record_outcome() when skip_reason contains 'graduated_rule_veto'. After next restart, rules will auto-retire at accuracy < 35% after 10 counterfactual outcomes.
